question.text: Which of the following order types is currently NOT available on the Indian Exchange trading system as per SEBI directives?

answer.text: AON (All or None) and MF (Minimum Fill) orders

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text explicitly notes: "Currently, AON and MF orders are not available on the system as per SEBI directives." Similarly: "Currently, GTC and GTD orders are not available on the system as per SEBI directives." **The Trap:** IOC, DQ, and Stop Loss orders are all currently available and actively used. The withdrawn categories are AON, MF, GTC, and GTD — all removed due to SEBI directives. **Cross-Exam Context:** Available order types: Market (with/without protection), Limit, Stop Loss, IOC, DQ, Cancel on Logout (COL). Withdrawn: AON, MF, GTC, GTD. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The "not available" orders are AON, MF, GTC, GTD. A useful memory aid: "AMGG" = the forbidden four by SEBI directive.
